name: MITRE ATT&CK MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for querying MITRE ATT&CK framework data — adversarial tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs) used by threat actors. Enables AI security agents to retrieve ATT&CK technique details, tactic mappings, threat group information, and mitigation guidance for security analysis and threat intelligence workflows.
use_cases: Querying ATT&CK techniques and tactics for threat intelligence analysis agents
Mapping observed attacker behaviors to ATT&CK framework from SOC agents
Retrieving threat group profiles and associated TTPs for threat hunting agents
Looking up mitigation guidance for specific ATT&CK techniques from security agents
not_for: Real-time threat detection (reference data, not live threat feeds)
Replacing full threat intelligence platforms for enterprise SOC
best_when: A security analyst or SOC team wants AI agents to quickly look up ATT&CK framework information — enabling natural language queries against the authoritative adversary behavior framework.
avoid_when: You need real-time threat intelligence or live threat feeds. ATT&CK is a reference framework — combine with threat intel feeds for operational use.

auth.notes: No auth required. MITRE ATT&CK data is publicly available. Likely queries local STIX data or the public ATT&CK API.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — MITRE ATT&CK is publicly available
pricing.notes: MITRE ATT&CK is free public knowledge base. MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: Local data or public API — minimal rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: ATT&CK framework version matters — ensure data is current (ATT&CK updates regularly)
Technique IDs change between ATT&CK versions — verify ID format matches your version
Community implementation — validate ATT&CK data accuracy against official MITRE sources
ATT&CK knowledge alone doesn't constitute threat intelligence — combine with live feeds
agent_readiness.security_notes: Public threat intelligence reference data. No sensitive data involved. Useful for defensive security research and SOC workflows. Verify ATT&CK data currency.

security_score_components.security_notes: Public ATT&CK reference data. No auth required. Defensive security use case. No sensitive data exposure.
